meta.domain_hint: Generic protocol change rules for structured messages/packets (protocol-agnostic)

changes.change_condition: for payloads sent from the client to the server in the example
changes.change_action: must be present only for record layer payloads containing the Finished message or application data
changes.evidence: only the record layer payloads containing the Finished message or application data include a CID.

changes.change_condition: if implementation chooses to receive CIDs of different lengths
changes.evidence: If, however, an implementation chooses to receive CIDs of different lengths, the assigned CID values must be self-delineating

changes.change_condition: in the example ServerHello extension
changes.evidence: ServerHello (connection_id=100)

changes.change_condition: in the example exchange, to indicate that a zero-length CID is present in the "connection_id" extension
changes.evidence: To indicate that a zero-length CID is present in the "connection_id" extension, we use the notation 'connection_id=empty'.

changes.change_condition: once encryption is enabled in the example exchange
changes.related_state_or_step: record layer after encryption is enabled
changes.evidence: In the example exchange, the CID is included in the record layer once encryption is enabled.

changes.change_condition: when the extension has been negotiated
changes.change_action: set to the negotiated CID value; length is cid_length bytes
changes.evidence: cid:  The CID value, cid_length bytes long, as agreed at the time the extension has been negotiated.

changes.change_condition: for all the pre-existing entries in the registry
changes.evidence: All the pre-existing entries are given the value "N".

changes.change_condition: for the connection_id(54) entry in the TLS ExtensionType Values registry
changes.evidence: | 54    |connection_id  | CH, | Y         | N           | RFC 9146  |

changes.change_condition: for the new registry column added to the "TLS ExtensionType Values" registry
changes.new_value: "Y" if the extension is only applicable to DTLS, "N" otherwise
changes.evidence: The valid entries are "Y" if the extension is only applicable to DTLS, "N" otherwise.

changes.change_condition: after DTLSInnerPlaintext is serialized
changes.change_action: set to encrypted form of the serialized DTLSInnerPlaintext
changes.evidence: enc_content:  The encrypted form of the serialized DTLSInnerPlaintext structure.

changes.change_condition: when determining whether a received datagram can trigger a peer address change
changes.change_action: must be newer than the newest datagram received
changes.old_value: epoch of the newest datagram received
changes.new_value: a greater/newer epoch in the received datagram
changes.evidence: The received datagram is "newer" (in terms of both epoch and sequence number) than the newest datagram received.

changes.change_condition: validation
changes.evidence: The length MUST NOT exceed 2^14.

changes.change_condition: when DTLSInnerPlaintext is serialized
changes.change_action: set to the length in bytes of the serialized DTLSInnerPlaintext
changes.evidence: length_of_DTLSInnerPlaintext:  The length (in bytes) of the serialized DTLSInnerPlaintext (two-byte integer).

changes.change_condition: MAC/additional_data construction for records carrying a CID
changes.change_action: used as tls12_cid in the constructed input
changes.evidence: seq_num_placeholder + tls12_cid + cid_length + tls12_cid + DTLSCiphertext.version + epoch + sequence_number + cid + length_of_DTLSInnerPlaintext;

changes.change_condition: for a DTLSCiphertext record carrying a CID
changes.evidence: outer_type:  The outer content type of a DTLSCiphertext record carrying a CID is always set to tls12_cid(25).

changes.change_condition: for MAC/additional_data construction
changes.evidence: seq_num_placeholder:  8 bytes of 0xff.

changes.change_condition: when determining whether a received datagram can trigger a peer address change
changes.change_action: must be newer than the newest datagram received
changes.old_value: sequence number of the newest datagram received
changes.new_value: a greater/newer sequence number in the received datagram
changes.evidence: The received datagram is "newer" (in terms of both epoch and sequence number) than the newest datagram received.

changes.change_condition: for applicability of this content type
changes.change_action: must be present only for DTLS 1.2
changes.evidence: The tls12_cid content type is only applicable to DTLS 1.2.

changes.change_condition: when registered in the "TLS ContentType" registry
changes.evidence: IANA has allocated tls12_cid(25) in the "TLS ContentType" registry.

changes.change_condition: when the record does not carry a CID
changes.change_action: must not be tls12_cid; records with other content types are excluded from the modified algorithm
changes.evidence: The modified algorithm MUST NOT be applied to records that do not carry a CID, i.e., records with content type other than tls12_cid.

changes.change_condition: may appear after the type field in cleartext
changes.change_action: set to an arbitrary-length run of zero-valued bytes
changes.evidence: zeros:  An arbitrary-length run of zero-valued bytes may appear in the cleartext after the type field.

changes.change_condition: used as DTLSInnerPlaintext padding
changes.evidence: An arbitrary-length run of zero-valued bytes may appear in the cleartext after the type field.
